hypothesis: If a convolutional neural network is first pretrained on a data-rich domain of additive-free SEM images before fine-tuning on a data-poor domain of additive-containing samples, then the resulting transfer learning model will achieve significantly higher classification accuracy for lithium-ion battery cathode composition and aging state identification compared to models trained without this two-stage pretraining strategy, because morphological features learned from the source domain provide transferable representations that compensate for the limited labeled data in the target domain.
